title_en: Nikah and Family Law — Marriage and Divorce
content_en:
This chapter covers Islamic family law — nikah (marriage), its conditions and pillars, and the related rulings of divorce, waiting periods, and custody. Sheikh al-Fawzan begins with the Quranic and Prophetic encouragement to marry, presenting marriage as a Sunnah that protects chastity, produces righteous children, and organizes society according to the divine design.

The chapter explains the essential pillars of a valid Islamic marriage: the offer and acceptance (ijab and qabul), the wali (guardian) of the bride, and the presence of witnesses. The Hanbali position that the wali is a condition for validity is argued with reference to the hadith: 'There is no nikah without a wali' (Ahmad and others). Sheikh al-Fawzan explains who may serve as a wali in order of priority, what happens when the wali is absent or unjustly refuses, and the rulings on the wali of a convert or of a woman whose male guardian has died.

The mahr (bridal gift) is covered as an obligatory right of the wife — not a payment to her father but a gift to her personally from the husband. The chapter discusses prompt and deferred mahr, the rulings when mahr is not specified at the time of contract (mahr al-mithl), and the wife's right to mahr in case of divorce before consummation.

Talaq (divorce) is presented with its categories: raj'i (revocable, allowing the husband to return during the iddah), ba'in (irrevocable without a new contract), and the triple divorce. Sheikh al-Fawzan addresses the contested question of the triple divorce pronounced in one sitting, presenting the Hanbali view and the positions of other scholars. Khul' — the wife's right to initiate dissolution of the marriage in exchange for returning the mahr — is explained as an important protection for women. The chapter concludes with rulings on the iddah (waiting period after divorce or death), maintenance obligations during iddah, and the basics of child custody (hadanah), which prioritizes the mother for young children before transferring to the father.
title_id: Nikah dan Hukum Keluarga — Pernikahan dan Perceraian
content_id:
Bab ini membahas salah satu bagian terpenting fikih: hukum keluarga Islam. Syekh al-Fawzan menyajikan dengan jelas hukum-hukum pernikahan, perceraian, dan semua yang berkaitan dengan kehidupan keluarga Muslim sesuai syariat.

Pembahasan nikah mencakup: anjuran menikah dan hikmahnya, syarat-syarat akad nikah, wali nikah dan syaratnya, mahar dan ketentuannya, larangan-larangan pernikahan (mahram), nikah mut'ah dan hukumnya, nikah misyar, walimah pernikahan. Pembahasan talak mencakup: talak dan jenisnya (raj'i, ba'in), hukum talak dalam berbagai kondisi, khul', ila', zhihar dan kafaratnya, li'an, iddah (wajib bagi perempuan yang dicerai atau ditinggal mati suaminya), dan hak hadanah (pengasuhan anak). Pembahasan penutup mencakup dasar-dasar faraid (ilmu waris) sesuai mazhab Hanbali.
